LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO DEFENDANT SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS RICHARD TEED AND WILLIAM HAZE, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND DENIES THE MOTION IN ITS ENTIRETY. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT THE COURT CAN CONSIDER THE MATERIALS RELIED ON BY SOTHEBY'S FOR ITS MOTION THAT ARE NOT ALLEGED IN OR ATTACHED TO THE FAC, ALL ALLEGATIONS SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN ARE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED. AS TO THE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING SOTHEBY'S FAILURE TO OBTAIN THE ERRORS AND OMISSIONS INSURANCE POLICY, THE ICA IS REASONABLY SUSCEPTIBLE TO PLAINTIFFS' PROFFERED INTERPRETATION OF THE ICA. AS TO THE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING THE ADMINISTRATIVE FEE, PLAINTIFFS ADEQUATELY ALLEGE THAT THE PARTIES' CONTRACT DOES NOT ALLOW FOR SUCH A FEE AND THUS THE IMPOSITION OF SUCH A FEE BREACHED THE PARTIES' CONTRACT. AS TO THE SOTHEBY'S STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ARGUMENTS, MR. SORIANO'S DECLARATION DOES NOT CLEARLY SHOW THAT ANY CLAIM OR PORTION THEREOF IS TIME-BARRED. IT IS UNCLEAR WHEN MR. TEED LEARNED THAT SOTHEBY'S FAILED TO OBTAIN THE ALLEGEDLY REQUIRED INSURANCE. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTIONS OF THE COURT'S RULING AND SUBMIT IT TO THE COURT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: G. GONZALES; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM; MTORREANO@SFTC.ORG. (302/HEK)

Off Calendar Aug-27-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL J. SMILKUN, ESQ. of Callagy Law (Tel: 201-261-1700) appearing via CourtCall for plaintiffs;
Atty. MICHAEL V. SHEPHERD, ESQ. of Wison Elser Moskowitz Edelman & Dicker LLP (Tel: 415-433-0990) appearing for defendant Sotheby's International Realty, Inc.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS RICHARD TEED AND William "Butch" Henry Haze's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY AND INJUNCTIVE RELEIF AND FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court modifies its tentative ruling to deny the motion without prejudice instead of ordering the motion off calendar. The Court rules as follows: Plaintiffs' motion for leave to file second amended complaint for declaratory and injunctive relief and first amended petition for writ of mandamus is denied without prejudice to renewal upon final disposition of the pending appeal. The case is stayed pending the disposition of the pending appeal from the court's order denying defendant Sotheby's International Realty, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration. (Code Civ. Proc., 916(a) ["the perfecting of an appeal stays proceedings in the trial court upon the judgment or order appealed from or upon the matters embraced therein or affected thereby"]; Varian Medical Systems, Inc. v. Delfino (2005) 35 Cal.4th 180, 190 ["an appeal from the denial of a motion to compel arbitration automatically stays all further trial court proceedings on the merits."].)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Peter Catalanotti, Esq. of Freeman Mathis & Gary LLP, T:415.260.1464, for defendant/moving party;
Brian Soriano, Esq. of the Law Offices of Brian Soriano, T:415.593.7333, for plaintiff/opposing party.

DEFENDANT SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALITY INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling, as follows:

Defendant Sotheby's International Realty Inc.'s motion to strike portions of the first amended complaint filed by plaintiffs Richard Teed and William Haze is denied in its entirety. Assuming without deciding that the court can consider the materials relied on by Sotheby's for its motion that are not alleged in or attached to the FAC, all allegations sought to be stricken are adequately alleged. As to the allegations regarding Sotheby's failure to obtain the errors and omissions insurance policy, the ICA is reasonably susceptible to plaintiffs' proffered interpretation of the ICA. As to the allegations regarding the administrative fee, plaintiffs adequately allege that the parties' contract does not allow for such a fee and thus the imposition of such a fee breached the parties' contract. As to the Sotheby's statute of limitations arguments, Mr. Soriano's declaration does not clearly show that any claim or portion thereof is time-barred. It is unclear when Mr. Teed learned that Sotheby's failed to obtain the allegedly required insurance.

Counsel for plaintiffs is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the court's ruling and submit it to the court in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.
